generated_pair.question: What HTTP status code does HCP Terraform return if an authentication token is absent or invalid?
supporting_sentences: If the token is absent or invalid, HCP Terraform responds with HTTP status 401 and a JSON API error object.

edited_pair.question: What HTTP status code does HCP Terraform return if an authentication token is absent or invalid?
supporting_sentences: If the token is absent or invalid, HCP Terraform responds with HTTP status 401 and a JSON API error object.

generated_pair.question: What are the four types of tokens that can be used to authenticate with the HCP Terraform API?
generated_pair.answer: User tokens, Team tokens, Organization tokens, and Audit trails token
supporting_sentences: You can use the following types of tokens to authenticate:
User tokens — each HCP Terraform user can have any number of API tokens, which can make requests on their behalf.
Team tokens — each team can have one API token at a time. This is intended for performing plans and applies via a CI/CD pipeline.

Organization tokens — each organization can have one API token at a time. This is intended for automating the management of teams, team membership, and workspaces. The organization token cannot perform plans and applies.
Audit trails token - each organization can have a single token that can read that organization's audit trails.

edited_pair.question: What are the four types of tokens that can be used to authenticate with the HCP Terraform API?
edited_pair.answer: User tokens, Team tokens, Organization tokens, and Audit trails token
supporting_sentences: You can use the following types of tokens to authenticate:
User tokens — each HCP Terraform user can have any number of API tokens, which can make requests on their behalf.
Team tokens — each team can have one API token at a time. This is intended for performing plans and applies via a CI/CD pipeline.

Organization tokens — each organization can have one API token at a time. This is intended for automating the management of teams, team membership, and workspaces. The organization token cannot perform plans and applies.
Audit trails token - each organization can have a single token that can read that organization's audit trails.

generated_pair.question: What is the maximum number of items per page that can be returned by a paginated HCP Terraform API endpoint?
supporting_sentences: page[size] Optional. If omitted, the endpoint will return 20 items per page. The maximum page size is 100.

supporting_sentences: policy-enforcement — Allows an organization to use Sentinel. Affects the policies, policy sets, and policy checks endpoints.

generated_pair.question: What is the default rate limit for most HCP Terraform API endpoints?
supporting_sentences: You can make up to 30 requests per second to most API endpoints as an authenticated or unauthenticated request.
